groups.canonical: Bitcoin as a decentralized digital currency without intermediaries
aliases: a decentralized digital currency without central banks or intermediaries
groups.rationale: Definition with no strict paraphrase match in the list.

groups.canonical: Bitcoin: A Peer-to-Peer Electronic Cash System
aliases: whitepaper titled "Bitcoin: A Peer-to-Peer Electronic Cash System"
groups.rationale: Reference to a specific work; no equivalent reference entry.

groups.canonical: Bitcoin creation by Satoshi Nakamoto in 2008
aliases: Bitcoin was created in 2008 by an anonymous person or group using the pseudonym Satoshi Nakamoto
groups.rationale: Historical claim with no strict paraphrase duplicate.

groups.canonical: Bitcoin blockchain throughput vs Visa
aliases: Bitcoin's blockchain processes roughly 7 transactions per second while Visa handles around 24,000
groups.rationale: Quantitative comparison not duplicated elsewhere.

groups.canonical: Hash-linked block structure
aliases: Each block contains the cryptographic hash of the previous block, creating an immutable chain
groups.rationale: Technical claim with no strict paraphrase match.

groups.canonical: Bitcoin block reward halves about every four years
aliases: The Bitcoin block reward halves approximately every four years
groups.rationale: Distinct issuance-schedule claim with no duplicate.

groups.canonical: Proof of Work mining puzzle competition
aliases: In Proof of Work, miners compete to solve complex mathematical puzzles
groups.rationale: Method description; no same-category paraphrase present.

groups.canonical: Initial Bitcoin block reward was 50 BTC
aliases: The Bitcoin block reward started at 50 BTC
groups.rationale: Specific historical claim with no duplicate.

groups.canonical: Bitcoin uses Proof of Work
aliases: Bitcoin uses what is called Proof of Work
groups.rationale: Consensus-mechanism claim distinct from method descriptions and other claims.

groups.canonical: Ethereum proposal and launch timeline
aliases: Ethereum was proposed by Vitalik Buterin in 2013 and launched in 2015
groups.rationale: Historical timeline claim with no strict paraphrase duplicate.

groups.canonical: Ethereum energy consumption reduced by approximately 99.95%
aliases: The transition reduced Ethereum's energy consumption by approximately 99.95% according to the Ethereum Foundation
groups.rationale: Reported result distinct from the transition claim itself.

groups.canonical: Ethereum's transition from Proof of Work to Proof of Stake
aliases: In September 2022, Ethereum transitioned from Proof of Work to Proof of Stake
groups.rationale: Protocol-change claim with no same-category paraphrase.

groups.canonical: DeFi TVL peaked above $180 billion in late 2021 before falling
aliases: Total value locked in DeFi protocols peaked at over $180 billion in late 2021 before declining significantly
groups.rationale: Historical quantitative claim distinct from the TVL metric definition.

groups.canonical: Total value locked (TVL) in DeFi protocols
aliases: TVL in DeFi protocols
groups.rationale: Metric term with no equivalent metric entry.

groups.canonical: Decentralized finance (DeFi) applications
aliases: DeFi applications that let users lend, borrow, and trade without traditional financial intermediaries
groups.rationale: Definition entry with no strict paraphrase duplicate.

groups.canonical: Uniswap cumulative trading volume since launch
aliases: Uniswap has processed over $1.8 trillion in trading volume since its launch
groups.rationale: Quantitative claim not duplicated elsewhere.

groups.canonical: Uniswap launch and AMM pioneering
aliases: Uniswap, launched in 2018, pioneered the automated market maker model
groups.rationale: Historical claim distinct from trading-volume claims.

groups.canonical: SEC classification of most cryptocurrencies as securities, excluding Bitcoin and Ethereum
aliases: The SEC classified most cryptocurrencies as securities, with exceptions for Bitcoin and Ethereum
groups.rationale: Regulatory claim with no strict paraphrase duplicate.

groups.canonical: Programmatic token sales on exchanges may not be securities transactions
aliases: Programmatic sales of tokens on exchanges do not necessarily constitute securities transactions
groups.rationale: Legal result distinct from the case reference itself.

groups.canonical: SEC v. Ripple Labs
aliases: SEC v. Ripple case in 2023
